decision_summary: The design is feasible with a chip max temperature of 58.6°C, well below the 85°C constraint. To further minimize the objective, I will increase the heat spreader conductivity. This is the highest-priority lever for improving lateral heat spreading and reducing peak temperature.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity enhances lateral heat distribution, lowering the localized peak temperature on the chip.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature profile across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur as conductivity becomes very high relative to other materials
Physical realism of 1000 W/(m*K) should be noted (exceeds typical copper, approaches diamond/Graphene range)
